audioscript.text: Section 1, you will hear a telephone conversation between a woman and the reservations clerk at a conference center.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: First, you have some time to look at questions 1 to 5.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You will see that there is an example that has been done for you.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: On this occasion only, the conversation relating to this will be played first.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Conference center reservations.
audioscript.speaker: CLERK

audioscript.text: Good morning.
audioscript.speaker: CLERK

audioscript.text: Hi, I'm interested in the Computing Conference next month.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Future directions in computing?
audioscript.speaker: CLERK

audioscript.text: Yes, that's right.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: The title of the course is Future Directions in computing.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: So, future directions has been written in the space.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Now we shall begin.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You should answer the questions as you listen, because you will not hear the recording a second time.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Listen carefully, and answer questions 1 to 5.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Conference center reservations.
audioscript.speaker: CLERK

audioscript.text: Good morning.
audioscript.speaker: CLERK

audioscript.text: Hi, I'm interested in the Computing Conference next month.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Future directions in computing?
audioscript.speaker: CLERK

audioscript.text: Yes, that's right.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Could I ask you a few questions about it?
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Of course.
audioscript.speaker: CLERK

audioscript.text: OK, I know the conference is for 3 days, but actually I want to attend on the Friday and Saturday only.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Will that work out to be any cheaper?
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Let me have a look.
audioscript.speaker: CLERK

audioscript.text: Well, you could register for the 2 days separately, but that wouldn't actually save you very much, as it still costs £35 for each day, in fact.
audioscript.speaker: CLERK

audioscript.text: If you could register for the three days, you also get an invitation to a free dinner on the Saturday night.
audioscript.speaker: CLERK

audioscript.text: So that's probably the better option.
audioscript.speaker: CLERK

audioscript.text: Right, I'll do that.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: How much will the fees be in that case?
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: All right. I'd like to register for the full 3 days.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Now can I pay that by credit card?
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: I'm afraid not.
audioscript.speaker: CLERK

audioscript.text: You'll have to send a cheque to us, or you can pay at the conference office.
audioscript.speaker: CLERK

audioscript.text: Fine, so it's probably easiest if I pay by cheque.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Now then, what else do I need to arrange?
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Right, how about accommodation?
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: I guess that's not included in the price.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: No, I'm sorry, it's not.
audioscript.speaker: CLERK

audioscript.text: But we do have a few rooms available for delegates at the conference center, if you'd like.
audioscript.speaker: CLERK

audioscript.text: Those are very cheap, but if you're interested, you'll need to book soon, because there's always a heavy demand for them.
audioscript.speaker: CLERK

audioscript.text: They are only £15 per night, but they are very basic, and you'd have to get your own breakfast, because they don't provide you with that, but it's very convenient.
audioscript.speaker: CLERK

audioscript.text: Because it's in the same building as the conference rooms.
audioscript.speaker: CLERK

audioscript.text: Uh-huh.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Or there's a very reasonable guest house, which is £25 per night, and I think that includes your breakfast.
audioscript.speaker: CLERK

audioscript.text: Is it close to the conference center?
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: It would be about a 10 minute walk away from here.
audioscript.speaker: CLERK

audioscript.text: I see.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: That sounds quite reasonable.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Before you hear the rest of the conversation, you have some time to look at questions 6 to 10.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Now listen and answer questions 6 to 10.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: The details are all in our conference pack, which I'll send you.
audioscript.speaker: CLERK

audioscript.text: Great, thanks.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: That'll be very useful.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Oh, and can you also send me an application form?
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Of course, I'll get that in the post to you straight away.
audioscript.speaker: CLERK

audioscript.text: Is there anything else?
audioscript.speaker: CLERK

audioscript.text: Yes, actually.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Can you tell me where exactly the conference center is?
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Well, it's on South Park Road, and it's right at the end of the road next to the library.
audioscript.speaker: CLERK

audioscript.text: It's a 10 minute taxi ride from the station, and will cost you £5.
audioscript.speaker: CLERK

audioscript.text: Otherwise you can take the bus which runs every half an hour from the station.
audioscript.speaker: CLERK

audioscript.text: That's the 21A, and it brings you straight to the conference center.
audioscript.speaker: CLERK

audioscript.text: Right, got that.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: That is the end of section 1.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You now have half a minute to check your answers.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Section 2, you will hear an introductory talk to a group of temporary workers at an international travel exhibition.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: First, you have some time to look at questions 11 to 13.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Now listen carefully and answer questions 11 to 13.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Good morning and welcome everybody.
audioscript.speaker: JENNY STEWART

audioscript.text: I'm Jenny Stewart, and I'm the staff manager here at the exhibition center.
audioscript.speaker: JENNY STEWART

audioscript.text: We're expecting this year's International Travel Exhibition to attract over 10,000 visitors a day, ladies and gentlemen.
audioscript.speaker: JENNY STEWART

audioscript.text: And you are among the 200 extra staff recruited to help look after them.
audioscript.speaker: JENNY STEWART

audioscript.text: Now, to help things run smoothly, we have divided you into four teams.
audioscript.speaker: JENNY STEWART

audioscript.text: The blue team, the green team, the red team, and the yellow team.
audioscript.speaker: JENNY STEWART

audioscript.text: So first, I'll explain how the teams are divided up, and then we'll be giving you color-coded T-shirts, so we can identify you more easily.
audioscript.speaker: JENNY STEWART

audioscript.text: First of all, those who will be looking after the phones and handling all calls regarding the exhibition, you will be on the red team.
audioscript.speaker: JENNY STEWART

audioscript.text: Now, we've also put the entrance staff on the red team.
audioscript.speaker: JENNY STEWART

audioscript.text: And you'll be stamping the entrance tickets and giving out publicity leaflets, OK?
audioscript.speaker: JENNY STEWART

audioscript.text: Those of you involved in distributing entrance tickets will be on the yellow team.
audioscript.speaker: JENNY STEWART

audioscript.text: And we've also put those of you who'll be staffing the information booths around the conference center on the yellow team, so you'll be getting a yellow T-shirt.
audioscript.speaker: JENNY STEWART

audioscript.text: Now most of the hospitality staff have been put in the blue team, so the chefs among you and the kitchen hands will all need a blue T-shirt, but, because of the sheer numbers, all waiting staff will be on the yellow team, and this includes the bar staff among you.
audioscript.speaker: JENNY STEWART

audioscript.text: Those who will be monitoring and directing the traffic in the car parks are on the green team, so you'll need to get a green T-shirt.
audioscript.speaker: JENNY STEWART

audioscript.text: This year, we've also employed a considerable number of attendants to direct the human traffic around the conference center.
audioscript.speaker: JENNY STEWART

audioscript.text: Now, you'll be working in the exhibition hall at all times, giving directions and generally helping people whenever you can, and you will be in the red team, so please collect a red T-shirt.
audioscript.speaker: JENNY STEWART

audioscript.text: Before you hear the rest of the talk, you have some time to look at questions 14 to 20.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Now listen and answer questions 14 to 20.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Right, now that everyone knows what team they're in, we'll get on with the orientation and training program, and first, I'll run through the rest of today's program, which you should have in front of you, so that you can get a general idea of what else is in store today.
audioscript.speaker: JENNY STEWART

audioscript.text: This introduction finishes at 9:30, and then you'll be hearing from Anne Smith.
audioscript.speaker: JENNY STEWART

audioscript.text: Anne works in the accounts department on level 2, and she looks after all temporary staff, and so she is the person to see if you have any problems regarding pay.
audioscript.speaker: JENNY STEWART

audioscript.text: Anne will be explaining when and how you get this.
audioscript.speaker: JENNY STEWART

audioscript.text: She will also be handing out your tax forms, and I will just stress that all tax forms must be completed and returned to Anne.
audioscript.speaker: JENNY STEWART

audioscript.text: Before your pay can be processed.
audioscript.speaker: JENNY STEWART

audioscript.text: Following Anne's talk, you'll hear from Peter Chen, our conference manager, and he will be going over a plan of the conference center with you, which will help you to orient yourselves.
audioscript.speaker: JENNY STEWART

audioscript.text: He will also go through the security arrangements with you, and show you the fire exits.
audioscript.speaker: JENNY STEWART

audioscript.text: Then at 10:30, there will be a coffee break in the staff canteen, which is located down on the ground floor.
audioscript.speaker: JENNY STEWART

audioscript.text: Now, after the break, we'll be moving to a different location for a video presentation.
audioscript.speaker: JENNY STEWART

audioscript.text: So, instead of coming back here, we'd like you all to go to lecture room 3 1 1.
audioscript.speaker: JENNY STEWART

audioscript.text: You'll find it on this same level, just down the hall.
audioscript.speaker: JENNY STEWART

audioscript.text: And there, you will be shown a video about important safety issues in the workplace called Safety at Work.
audioscript.speaker: JENNY STEWART

audioscript.text: We'll finish off the morning with lunch.
audioscript.speaker: JENNY STEWART

audioscript.text: Which should give everyone a chance to meet and get to know each other, and we'll be serving you a buffet lunch in the Main Hall, which is on the first floor from 12 o'clock.
audioscript.speaker: JENNY STEWART

audioscript.text: After lunch, you'll be divided up into your teams to meet your team leaders, and we're hoping to be all finished by 3.00, so I won't delay you any longer, and I'll hand over to...
audioscript.speaker: JENNY STEWART

audioscript.text: That is the end of section 2.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You now have half a minute to check your answers.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

body.title: Travel Expo

test_text.title: Student's Inquiries about the School of Education Libraries

audioscript.text: Section 3, you will hear a conversation between a librarian and an education student.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: First, you have some time to look at questions 21 to 27.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Now listen carefully and answer questions 21 to 27.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Good afternoon.
audioscript.speaker: LIBRARIAN

audioscript.text: Can I help you?
audioscript.speaker: LIBRARIAN

audioscript.text: Good afternoon.
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: Yes, I've just transferred to the School of Education, and I'd like information about joining the library.
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: Well, the School of Education has libraries on two sites, as I'm sure you know.
audioscript.speaker: LIBRARIAN

audioscript.text: This one here is the Fordham site, and the other is on Castle Road.
audioscript.speaker: LIBRARIAN

audioscript.text: And is there any difference between the two libraries?
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: Not in terms of their facilities.
audioscript.speaker: LIBRARIAN

audioscript.text: Access to online databases and the Internet is available at both sites, and each site has a range of reference materials on education.
audioscript.speaker: LIBRARIAN

audioscript.text: Oh, yes, I see.
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: But the Castle Road site has books on the sociology of education, and a collection of textbooks and teaching resources covering most of the subjects taught in secondary schools.
audioscript.speaker: LIBRARIAN

audioscript.text: Ah. Right.
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: But I'm training to be a primary teacher, so I need to look at materials for the 5 to 11 age group.
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: Then you've come to the right place. At Fordham, we hold material relating to primary education, as well as special needs.
audioscript.speaker: LIBRARIAN

audioscript.text: But of course you'll need to familiarize yourself with both sites to make the most of our resources.
audioscript.speaker: LIBRARIAN

audioscript.text: You haven't mentioned periodicals.
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: Are they held at both sites?
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: Current issues, yes.
audioscript.speaker: LIBRARIAN

audioscript.text: But if you want to look at back issues, you'll need to use the CD-ROM databases, which are held here at Fordham.
audioscript.speaker: LIBRARIAN

audioscript.text: I see.
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: Now, about borrowing books, I'm living out of town, so I'm hoping I can borrow quite a few items, and cut down on the number of trips I have to make.
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: Right, well members can borrow two books at a time from each site.
audioscript.speaker: LIBRARIAN

audioscript.text: Only two books, only two from each site, but that's four altogether.
audioscript.speaker: LIBRARIAN

audioscript.text: Oh, I see.
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: And how long can I hang on to them for?
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: The borrowing period is one month, but of course books can be renewed.
audioscript.speaker: LIBRARIAN

audioscript.text: You can renew any item a maximum of 3 times.
audioscript.speaker: LIBRARIAN

audioscript.text: Hmm. Do I have to come to the library to do that?
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: No, you can do it by telephone or email, but you can't renew overdue books this way.
audioscript.speaker: LIBRARIAN

audioscript.text: Uh. Only before or on the due date stamped in the book.
audioscript.speaker: LIBRARIAN

audioscript.text: We'll need your full name, your borrower number, and the name of the site library you borrowed the items from.
audioscript.speaker: LIBRARIAN

audioscript.text: So theoretically, I can borrow books for up to 3 months.
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: Is that what you're saying?
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: Yes, provided they're not recalled.
audioscript.speaker: LIBRARIAN

audioscript.text: So what happens then?
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: Well, sometimes an item is requested by another borrower, in which case we'd send you a letter, and you'd have to return the book within 7 working days.
audioscript.speaker: LIBRARIAN

audioscript.text: Don't forget we're closed on Sundays.
audioscript.speaker: LIBRARIAN

audioscript.text: OK.
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: Before you hear the rest of the conversation, you have some time to look at questions 28 to 30.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Now listen and answer questions 28 to 30.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: I expect it'll take me a while to find what I need.
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: There's such a lot here.
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: Yes, there is.
audioscript.speaker: LIBRARIAN

audioscript.text: But if you need help getting started, this term we're running 3 study skills workshops.
audioscript.speaker: LIBRARIAN

audioscript.text: Oh?
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: What are they on?
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: Er, let me see. The first one's on resources.
audioscript.speaker: LIBRARIAN

audioscript.text: Um. Yes, here it is.
audioscript.speaker: LIBRARIAN

audioscript.text: How to use the library's resources.
audioscript.speaker: LIBRARIAN

audioscript.text: That includes everything, not just the print and technical resources.
audioscript.speaker: LIBRARIAN

audioscript.text: That sounds useful.
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: Hmm. Is there anything on using the Internet?
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: Uh. Let's see.
audioscript.speaker: LIBRARIAN

audioscript.text: The one on the Internet for beginners was last term, this term it's finding research materials online.
audioscript.speaker: LIBRARIAN

audioscript.text: That sounds Interesting too.
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: Hmm. What's the last one?
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: It's a workshop on dissertations.
audioscript.speaker: LIBRARIAN

audioscript.text: What do you mean?
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: How to write one?
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: Uh. No, it's more to do with academic writing conventions.
audioscript.speaker: LIBRARIAN

audioscript.text: You know, writing a bibliography, and how to refer to sources in your text.
audioscript.speaker: LIBRARIAN

audioscript.text: That sort of thing.
audioscript.speaker: LIBRARIAN

audioscript.text: Anyway, here's a leaflet with information about all three.
audioscript.speaker: LIBRARIAN

audioscript.text: Uh. Thanks a lot.
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: That'll be very useful.
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: You're welcome.
audioscript.speaker: LIBRARIAN

audioscript.text: That is the end of section 3.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You now have half a minute to check your answers.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

items: The libraries on both sites provide internet access and have a variety of <input> materials on education.
The Castle Road library has books on sociology, together with <input> and other resources relevant to the majority of <input> school subjects.
The Fordham library includes resources for teaching in <input> education and special needs.
Current issues of periodicals are available at both libraries, although <input> issues are only available at Fordham.

desc.constraint: NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS AND/OR A NUMBER

items.title: Which books cannot be renewed by telephone or email?
How much time is allowed to return recalled books?

body.title: Which THREE topics do this term's study skills workshops cover?
options: How to carry out research for a dissertation
Making good use of the whole range of library services
Standard requirements when writing a dissertation
Using the Internet when doing research
What books and technical resources are available in the library

test_text.title: Asiatic Lions in the Gir Sanctuary

audioscript.text: Section 4, you will hear part of a lecture given by a conservationist on the subject of lions.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: First, you have some time to look at questions 31 to 40.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Now listen carefully and answer questions 31 to 40.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Well, most people think that lions only come from Africa, and you would be forgiven for thinking this, because in fact most lions do come from Africa.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: But this hasn't always been the case.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: If we go back 10,000 years, we would find that there were lions roaming vast sections of the globe. But now, unfortunately, only very small sections of the lions' former habitat remain.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: My particular interest is Asiatic lions, which are a subspecies of African lions.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: It's almost 100,000 years since the Asiatic lion split off and developed as a subspecies.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: At one time, the Asiatic lion was living as far west as Greece, and they were found from there, in a band that spread east through various countries of the Middle East, all the way to India.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: In museums, you can now see Greek coins that have clear images of the Asiatic lion on them.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Most of them are dated at around 500 B.C.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: However, Europe saw its last Asiatic lion roaming free 2000 years ago.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Over the next nineteen hundred years, the numbers of Asiatic lions in the other areas declined steadily.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: But it was only in the 19th century that they disappeared from everywhere but India.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: So, how can you tell an Asiatic lion from an African lion, with which you're probably more familiar?
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Well, in general, Asiatic lions are not as big as African lions.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: The color is more or less the same, but the appearance of the mane is different,
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: that's the hair around the lion's face and neck.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: The Asiatic lion's mane is noticeably shorter than the African lion's.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Asiatic lions also have a long fold of skin on their undersides, whereas not many African lions have this.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Well, I'd like to talk to you now about the Gir Sanctuary in India.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: That's where I've just come back from.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: The sanctuary was established specifically to protect the Asiatic lion.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: It's 1,450 square kilometers in area, and most of it is forest.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: There are now around 300 Asiatic lions in India, and almost all of them are in the sanctuary.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: But despite living in a sanctuary, which makes them safe from hunters, they still face a number of problems that threaten their survival.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: One of these is the ever present danger of disease.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: This is what killed more than a third of Africa's Serengeti lions in 1994, and people are fearful that something similar could happen in the Gir Sanctuary, and kill off many of the Asiatic lions there.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: India's lions are particularly vulnerable because they have a limited gene pool.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: The reason for this is interesting.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: It's because all of them are descended from a few dozen lions that were saved by a prince who took a particular interest in them.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: He was very wealthy, and he managed to protect them.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Otherwise they'd probably have died out completely.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: When you see the Asiatic lion in India, what you sense is enormous vitality.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: They're very impressive beasts, and you would never guess that they have this vulnerability when you look at them.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: The Asiatic lions don't have the Gir Sanctuary to themselves, I should add.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: They actually share it with about 2,000 farmers.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: A significant proportion of the lions' diet is made up of the livestock of these farmers.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Goats, chickens and so on, as much as a third in fact.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: And they've even been known to attack humans, especially in times of drought.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: One final piece of interesting information, in ancient India, one of the greatest tests of leadership for a man was to fight a lion.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Now it seems in modern India, it will be a great test to see if the lion can be saved.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: I'm sure this is something that all of you will share concern for too.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: That is the end of Section 4.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You now have half a minute to check your answers.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER
